title: GCBF future round watch
notify: Not-for-profit clubs, community groups, sport groups, arts groups and auspiced informal groups.
tip: Round 126 is closed. The next GCBF round date is still not announced while Queensland introduces a new online grants system. This Round 126 note applies only to GCBF.
action: Use the waiting period to confirm eligibility, auspice, quotes, public benefit, governance and reporting capacity.

title: Redland Council Community Support Grant Round 1 readiness
notify: Redland-based not-for-profits, community groups, environment groups, wildlife carers, clubs and local project leads.
tip: Round 1 for 2026/27 is scheduled for 27 July 2026 to 16 August 2026. Funding is up to $20,000 and applications are open quarterly.
action: Prepare applicant eligibility, ABN, insurance, quotes over $5,000, project plan, risk assessment, budget and reporting plan before 27 July.
source: Redland City Council Community Support Grant

title: Redland Council grants portal watch
notify: Local applicants who may fit council grants, RADF, events, rapid response or sponsorship programs.
tip: Council keeps multiple grant streams and support pages. The portal should be checked before assuming Community Support is the best fit.
action: Scan council program pages monthly and before any local event, arts, environment or community application.
source: Redland City Council Grants and Sponsorship

title: Multicultural infrastructure Batch 2
notify: Multicultural organisations, community facilities, arts spaces, community hubs and partnership projects with not-for-profit eligibility.
tip: Batch 2 is open until 4:00pm AEDT 12 November 2026. Streams range from $20,000 to $1.5 million and require co-contribution and land ownership or approval evidence.
action: Only shortlist if the applicant is eligible, not-for-profit, infrastructure-ready and has landowner evidence plus co-contribution capacity.
source: Australian Government Department of Home Affairs

title: First Nations clean energy advice window
notify: First Nations organisations or groups considering clean energy development, advice, FPIC, planning or technical support.
tip: Open until 5:00pm AEST 3 September 2026, unless funding is allocated earlier. Grants are $5,000 to $80,000.
action: Confirm First Nations applicant eligibility, ABN, project authority, advice provider, FPIC framing and whether the project needs quotes over $40,000.
source: business.gov.au First Nations Clean Energy Advice Grants

title: First Nations Digital Support Hub deadline
notify: First Nations digital inclusion partners with capacity for national or staged support delivery.
tip: Full applications are due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026. This is likely a large lead-provider or partnership opportunity, not a casual small grant.
action: Triage immediately: confirm eligibility, lead applicant, delivery capacity, cultural authority, partners and whether the island project is a partner rather than applicant.
source: Department of Infrastructure First Nations Digital Inclusion

title: Network of Digital Mentors deadline
notify: First Nations digital mentors, community media, local access, training and capability projects with eligible lead partners.
tip: Full applications are due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026. It could align with noticeboard, kiosks and community media, but only if eligibility and authority are real.
action: Treat as a rapid partner check, not a cold application: identify eligible lead, mentors, communities, training evidence and delivery budget.
source: Department of Infrastructure First Nations Digital Inclusion

title: PBC capacity closing check
notify: Native title holding corporations, PBCs and authorised First Nations governance bodies.
tip: Indigenous.gov.au lists this as closing 30 June 2026. It is only relevant if the applicant is the right kind of native-title corporation or has a proper role.
action: Check applicant status and authority before drafting. Do not bend a community project into a PBC grant without governance fit.

title: First Nations open grants scan
notify: First Nations organisations, cultural projects, Country-care projects and partner-led projects where cultural authority is clear.
tip: Indigenous.gov.au currently lists open and ongoing grants across Country, economic development, health, digital inclusion, arts and local investment.
action: Check who is actually eligible before drafting. Cultural authority is not a writing detail; it is a project foundation.

title: Queensland First Nations program scan
notify: Queensland First Nations organisations, cultural projects, wellbeing, languages, reconciliation, business, housing, sport and Country-care projects.
tip: Queensland lists multiple First Nations funding pathways including NAIDOC, languages, reconciliation, wellbeing, business, housing, arts, sport, Looking after Country and GenQ.
action: Use as a category scan, then confirm the live program page before drafting or notifying anyone.
source: Queensland First Nations grants and funding

title: First Nations housing support routing
notify: NSI Housing, aged-care providers, community service partners and housing advocacy projects.
tip: This is support-pathway context rather than a single open project grant. It helps separate renting, public/community housing, remote housing, emergency accommodation and aged-care housing issues.
action: Build a housing-specific evidence pack before looking for capital, maintenance, tenancy support or crisis-response funding.

title: Sport infrastructure and 2032 legacy scan
notify: Sport clubs, facility committees, surf, beach sport, youth and inclusive recreation projects.
tip: Queensland sport funding lists Active Clubs, First Nations sport, grassroots infrastructure, inclusive facilities, recovery and active industry programs.
action: Keep a sport facility evidence folder: site control, quotes, plans, participation evidence, inclusion benefits and maintenance responsibility.

title: Arts Queensland program scan
notify: Artists, arts organisations, festivals, touring, creative development and First Nations arts projects.
tip: Arts Queensland separates funding for individuals and organisations. Check specific arts program pages for actual open dates before creating a grant notice.
action: Keep artist profile.md, aura.md, business-profile.md and project evidence ready before matching a round.

title: Business support scan
notify: Local businesses, sole traders, artists, food producers, tourism operators and digital service providers.
tip: business.gov.au can filter by Queensland, industry, business structure, Indigenous business status, open/coming/closed, and needs such as AI, digital, disaster, equipment, events and training.
action: Use filters monthly and whenever a business-profile.md is created or updated.

title: Community Grants Hub open grants scan
notify: Community groups, not-for-profits, service providers and project leads that match an actual open Australian Government round.
tip: Community Grants Hub currently lists open grants and states GrantConnect is the authoritative grants information source.
action: Scan open grants, shortlist only genuine island-relevant opportunities, then confirm final rules on GrantConnect.

title: GrantConnect authority check
notify: Any applicant before a federal application is drafted or submitted.
tip: GrantConnect is the authoritative Australian Government grants source, but may require a browser/manual check. Do not rely only on summaries for final eligibility.
action: Before drafting, capture the GrantConnect GO number, close date, eligibility, attachments and guidelines link.

title: Health, disability and aged-care grant scan
notify: Yulu-Burri-Ba, Moopi, NSI Housing, Elders groups and partner services where authority is clear.
tip: The department points public health, disability and aged-care grants through GrantConnect and Community Grants Hub.
action: Prepare evidence of need, service authority, privacy boundaries, cultural safety and delivery partners before drafting.
source: Australian Government Department of Health, Disability and Ageing

title: QRA resilience programs scan
notify: Emergency readiness, resilience, infrastructure, local disaster, risk reduction and community safety projects.
tip: QRA lists current resilience funding programs including QRRRP, DRFA efficiencies, Protecting Our Communities, North Queensland resilience and DRF.
action: Map the project to risk reduction, recovery, resilience or emergency readiness before choosing a funding path.

title: Disaster support noticeboard routing
notify: Residents, businesses and services only when an activated disaster support pathway applies.
tip: Queensland disaster grants include emergency hardship, essential services, household contents, structural assistance and reconnection support. This is public support routing, not ordinary project funding.
action: Use for emergency notices and referrals after activated events; do not list as a generic project grant.
source: Queensland disaster grants and financial assistance

title: Queensland grants finder broad scan
notify: Any Queensland-based applicant before assuming a state grant does or does not exist.
tip: Use the Queensland Grants Finder as a broad search layer, then confirm the program page and round dates from the administering agency.
action: Run a filtered state scan for sport, arts, environment, First Nations, business, disaster and community keywords monthly.

title: Global environment and island resilience watch
notify: Environment, biodiversity, climate, land-and-sea, women-led, Indigenous peoples and research partnership projects.
tip: UNOPS small grants information is a global programme-support pathway, not a quick local round. It suits long preparation and partnerships.
action: Treat as partnership development first, application writing second.

title: Island sponsorship and partner support watch
notify: Businesses, venues, clubs, artists, project leads and community partners.
tip: Not every useful pathway is a government grant. Local support may be cash, in-kind, a venue, a letter, a noticeboard slot, a market table or a media partnership.
action: Build a local partner list beside each grant-ready project and record asks, offers and follow-up dates.
source: Island entities and local partner research
